title: Water Engineer
description: **Senior Civil Engineer (Water \& Drainage)** We are seeking a motivated and forward\-thinking Senior Civil Engineer with strong expertise in water and drainage infrastructure. This role suits an experienced engineer who excels at problem\-solving, delivering technically robust solutions, and mentoring others in a collaborative environment. A keen interest in sustainable urban drainage, water infrastructure, and integrated civil design is essential, as you will play a key role in delivering projects that enhance resilience, environmental performance, and the public realm while supporting the continued growth of our practice. **About the Company** Clifton Scannell Emerson Associates (CSEA) is a long\-established Civil, Structural and Transportation Engineering consultancy with over 70 years’ experience delivering high\-quality design and project management services across Ireland and internationally. We are trusted on major infrastructure and building projects, combining technical excellence with practical, buildable solutions. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer, we are committed to continuous professional development, mentoring, and career progression within a collaborative, team\-focused environment where people are supported to grow and succeed. Since 2010, CSEA has been at the forefront of Data Centre design, delivering projects across Ireland, the UK, and mainland Europe. We have successfully implemented Building Information Modelling (BIM) from concept through to handover on a vast number of projects. Currently, CSEA employs over 115 staff across our two offices. **About the Role** Lead the delivery of civil engineering design with a primary focus on water, wastewater, and drainage infrastructure across a range of projects, including urban development, active travel schemes, and public realm improvements. **Responsibilities** * Take responsibility for designs through all project stages — feasibility, preliminary, detailed design, and construction support. * Design and develop sustainable drainage systems (SuDS), stormwater networks, foul drainage, attenuation systems, watermain infrastructure, and integrated road/drainage solutions. * Prepare and review drawings, calculations, hydraulic models, and technical deliverables in accordance with relevant Irish and European standards and guidance (e.g. Irish Water standards, Greater Dublin Drainage Study, DMURS, CIRIA SuDS Manual, TII, etc.). * Coordinate effectively with multidisciplinary teams, including landscape architects, environmental specialists, structural engineers, and transport teams, to deliver integrated and sustainable urban solutions. * Provide technical guidance and mentoring to technicians and junior engineers in hydraulic design and drainage engineering. * Participate in client, stakeholder, and project team meetings. * Undertake site visits for surveys, inspections, and construction support as required. * Prepare technical reports, specifications, contract documentation, and flood risk assessments. * Support project planning and programming to ensure deadlines are met. * Liaise with Irish Water, local authorities, utility providers, and other stakeholders as part of the design and approval process. * Contribute to design reviews with a focus on safety, sustainability, climate resilience, water quality, and buildability. * Support the quality assurance process and ensure compliance with internal procedures. * Actively contribute to knowledge sharing and continuous improvement within the team. **Qualifications** * Minimum 10 years’ relevant experience working on civil engineering projects, with significant exposure to water, wastewater, and drainage design (including SuDS and urban drainage schemes). * Degree in Civil Engineering (Level 8 or equivalent). * Proven track record of successfully delivering water/drainage projects across multiple design stages. * Strong understanding of Irish design standards and guidance for water infrastructure (Irish Water, EPA, Local Authority requirements, DMURS, TII, CIRIA, etc.). **Required Skills** * Proficient in AutoCAD; experience with AutoCAD Civil 3D and hydraulic modelling software (e.g. MicroDrainage, InfoWorks, or similar) is highly advantageous. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills. * Demonstrated ability to manage workload, meet deadlines, and work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * Experience mentoring or guiding junior staff is desirable.
